title: Schelhammeria capitata

The Annonaceae family consists of around 200 genera and over 2,000 species, many of which are tropical or subtropical. Schelhammeria capitata is one of the lesser-known species within this family. Further classification details may be found through more in-depth botanical research.
uses: Schelhammeria capitata, also known as the Cape Jasmine or Cape Jessamine, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Some studies suggest that Schelhammeria capitata may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Further studies are required to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications.
